questions.question: What is the direction of magnetic field lines near the poles of a magnet?
questions.correct_answer: They enter and exit at right angles to the surface
questions.explanation: Magnetic field lines form closed loops around magnets, with lines entering and exiting at right angles to the surface near the poles.

questions.question: What is the term for a material that can be magnetized?
questions.explanation: Ferromagnetic materials are capable of being magnetized and exhibit strong magnetic properties.

questions.question: What is the term for a material that cannot be magnetized?
questions.explanation: Non-ferromagnetic materials do not have strong magnetic properties and cannot be magnetized.

questions.question: What is the unit of measurement for magnetic field strength?
questions.explanation: The Tesla (T) is the SI unit of measurement for magnetic field strength.

questions.question: What is the term for a device that converts mechanical energy into electrical energy?
questions.explanation: A generator is a device that converts mechanical energy into electrical energy through electromagnetic induction.

questions.question: What is the term for a device that converts electrical energy into mechanical energy?
questions.explanation: A motor is a device that converts electrical energy into mechanical energy through electromagnetic induction.

questions.question: What is the term for a type of magnet that can be easily demagnetized?
questions.explanation: Temporary magnets are made of materials that can be easily demagnetized and lose their magnetic properties when heated or exposed to strong magnetic fields.

questions.question: What is the term for a type of magnet that retains its magnetic properties?
questions.explanation: Permanent magnets are made of materials that retain their magnetic properties and cannot be easily demagnetized.

questions.question: What is the term for a device that measures the strength of a magnetic field?
questions.explanation: A magnetometer is a device used to measure the strength and direction of a magnetic field.

questions.question: What is the term for a phenomenon where a material becomes magnetized in the presence of an external magnetic field?
questions.explanation: Magnetic induction occurs when a material becomes magnetized in the presence of an external magnetic field.

questions.question: What is the term for a phenomenon where a material loses its magnetic properties?
questions.explanation: Demagnetization occurs when a material loses its magnetic properties due to various factors such as heat, vibration, or exposure to strong magnetic fields.

notes.title: Magnetic Field Lines
notes.content: Magnetic field lines form closed loops around magnets, with lines entering and exiting at right angles to the surface near the poles.

notes.title: Ferromagnetic Materials
notes.content: Ferromagnetic materials are capable of being magnetized and exhibit strong magnetic properties.

notes.title: Non-Ferromagnetic Materials
notes.content: Non-ferromagnetic materials do not have strong magnetic properties and cannot be magnetized.

notes.title: Magnetic Field Strength Unit
notes.content: The Tesla (T) is the SI unit of measurement for magnetic field strength.

notes.title: Generators and Motors
notes.content: A generator converts mechanical energy into electrical energy through electromagnetic induction, while a motor converts electrical energy into mechanical energy through electromagnetic induction.

notes.title: Temporary Magnets
notes.content: Temporary magnets are made of materials that can be easily demagnetized and lose their magnetic properties when heated or exposed to strong magnetic fields.

notes.title: Permanent Magnets
notes.content: Permanent magnets are made of materials that retain their magnetic properties and cannot be easily demagnetized.

notes.title: Magnetometers
notes.content: A magnetometer is a device used to measure the strength and direction of a magnetic field.

notes.title: Magnetic Induction
notes.content: Magnetic induction occurs when a material becomes magnetized in the presence of an external magnetic field.

notes.title: Demagnetization
notes.content: Demagnetization occurs when a material loses its magnetic properties due to various factors such as heat, vibration, or exposure to strong magnetic fields.

facts.fact: The Earth's magnetic field is weakening over time.
Magnetic field lines can be visualized using iron filings or a compass.
Permanent magnets retain their magnetic properties indefinitely if not demagnetized.
Magnetic fields can be created by moving charges, such as electric currents or changing magnetic fields.
The Earth's magnetic field has reversed many times throughout its history.
Magnetic induction is responsible for the operation of many electrical devices, including generators and motors.
Demagnetization can be caused by various factors, including heat, vibration, or exposure to strong magnetic fields.
Magnetic field strength is measured in Teslas (T).
The Earth's magnetic field is tilted at an angle of about 11 degrees from the Earth's rotational axis.
